chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# IVD Recipe: Self-Evaluating Workflow Pattern (Experimental)
# ==============================================================================
# Pattern for workflows that measure output quality, adjust within declared
# boundaries, and re-verify — extending IVD verification from pass/fail to
# continuous improvement.
#
# Status: Experimental
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-02-09
# ==============================================================================

recipe:
  name: "Self-Evaluating Workflow Pattern"
  version: "1.0"
  category: "workflow_patterns"
  status: "experimental"
  created: "2026-02-09"

  description: |
    Pattern for workflows that run repeatedly and should improve over iterations.
    Combines the evaluation section (continuous quality measurement) with the
    authorship section (who has authority to adjust) to create a safe, bounded
    improvement loop.

    The core loop:
    1. Execute workflow
    2. Evaluate output against quality criteria
    3. If criteria not met: propose adjustment (within declared scope)
    4. Verify adjustment doesn't violate constraints
    5. Apply adjustment and re-execute
    6. Repeat until criteria met, max_iterations reached, or escalation triggered

    This is distinct from standard IVD verification:
    - Verification = "does the output pass constraints?"   (binary: yes/no)
    - Evaluation   = "how good is the output? improve it." (continuous, bounded)

  problem_solved: |
    Workflows that run repeatedly often need quality improvement, but:
    - Improvement is ad-hoc ("make it better" with no structure)
    - No guardrails on what the improvement loop can change
    - No stopping condition (infinite iteration risk)
    - No escalation when the loop can't improve enough
    - No separation between what the AI can adjust vs. what needs human approval

  applies_to:
    - "ETL/data pipelines that should improve data quality over runs"
    - "AI classification workflows targeting precision/recall improvements"
    - "Content generation pipelines with quality scoring"
    - "Autonomous agent workflows that optimize their own process"
    - "Any repeatable workflow with measurable quality metrics"

  when_to_use:
    - "Workflow runs repeatedly and output quality is measurable"
    - "Quality should improve over iterations, not just pass/fail"
    - "Clear metrics exist (precision, latency, completeness, etc.)"
    - "Adjustment scope can be bounded (not everything is changeable)"
    - "Escalation path exists when automated improvement isn't enough"

  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "One-shot workflows with no iteration (just use constraints)"
    - "No measurable quality metrics (can't evaluate what you can't measure)"
    - "All changes require human approval (use standard verification instead)"
    - "Task-level intents (too granular for an improvement loop)"

# ==============================================================================
# TEMPLATE SECTIONS
# ==============================================================================

intent_template:
  summary: "[Workflow name] with self-evaluation f

able)"
    - "Escalation path exists when automated improvement isn't enough"

  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "One-shot workflows with no iteration (just use constraints)"
    - "No measurable quality metrics (can't evaluate what you can't measure)"
    - "All changes require human approval (use standard verification instead)"
    - "Task-level intents (too granular for an improvement loop)"

# ==============================================================================
# TEMPLATE SECTIONS
# ==============================================================================

intent_template:
  summary: "[Workflow name] with self-evaluation for [quality goal]"
  goal: "Execute [process], measure [metrics], and improve within [boundaries] over [max] iterations"
  success_metric: "Quality criteria met within max_iterations; no constraint violations; escalation rate < [threshold]"
  stakeholders:
    - "[Primary consumer of workflow output]"
    - "[Human escalation point]"

authorship_template:
  origin: "ai_proposed"
  human_oversight: "review_required"
  ai_authority:
    can_create: false
    can_modify: ["implementation", "workflow_steps", "verification"]
    requires_approval: ["intent.goal", "intent.success_metric", "constraints"]
  escalation: "When adjustment impacts constraints or quality cannot improve after max_iterations"

evaluation_template:
  criteria:
    - metric: "[primary_quality_metric]"
      target: ">= [target_value]"
      source: "automated"
    - metric: "[secondary_metric]"
      target: "[target_condition]"
      source: "automated"
    - metric: "constraint_pass_rate"
      target: "100%"
      source: "ivd_validate"

  adjustment:
    authority: "ai_proposed"
    scope:
      - "[what CAN be changed — e.g., model parameters, workflow steps, thresholds]"
    protected:
      - "intent.goal"
      - "intent.success_metric"
      - "constraints"
      - "[other fields that must not change]"

  cycle:
    trigger: "on_completion"
    max_iterations: 3
    stop_when: "All criteria met or max_iterations reached"
    escalate_when: "Cannot meet criteria after max_iterations"

constraints_template:
  - name: "evaluation_bounded"
    requirement: "Evaluation loop must terminate within max_iterations"
    test: "tests/test_evaluation.py::test_max_iterations_enforced"
    consequence_if_violated: "Infinite improvement loop — wasted compute, no convergence"

  - name: "protected_fields_enforced"
    requirement: "Evaluation loop cannot modify protected fields (constraints, goal, success_metric)"
    test: "tests/test_evaluation.py::test_protected_fields"
    consequence_if_violated: "Goal drift — the system optimizes for the wrong thing"

  - name: "constraints_never_relaxed"
    requirement: "Evaluation adjustments must not relax existing constraints"
    test: "tests/test_evaluation.py::test_constraints_preserved"
    consequence_if_violated: "Quality floor drops — output passes lower bar, not original requirement"

  - name: "escalation_triggered"
    requirement: "When max_iterations reached without meeting criteria, human is notified"
    test: "tests/test_evaluation.py::test_escalation"
    consequence_if_violated: "Silent failure

sts/test_evaluation.py::test_protected_fields"
    consequence_if_violated: "Goal drift — the system optimizes for the wrong thing"

  - name: "constraints_never_relaxed"
    requirement: "Evaluation adjustments must not relax existing constraints"
    test: "tests/test_evaluation.py::test_constraints_preserved"
    consequence_if_violated: "Quality floor drops — output passes lower bar, not original requirement"

  - name: "escalation_triggered"
    requirement: "When max_iterations reached without meeting criteria, human is notified"
    test: "tests/test_evaluation.py::test_escalation"
    consequence_if_violated: "Silent failure — system can't improve but no one knows"

rationale_template:
  decision: "[Why self-evaluation over manual improvement; why these criteria; why this adjustment scope]"
  reasoning: |
    Self-evaluation was chosen because:
    - [Workflow runs frequently enough to benefit from automated improvement]
    - [Quality metrics are measurable and automatable]
    - [Adjustment scope is well-defined — not everything needs to change]
    - [Human escalation provides safety net for cases beyond automated improvement]
  evidence: "[Link to quality data, baseline measurements, or pilot results]"
  date: "[YYYY-MM-DD]"
  stakeholder: "[Who approved the evaluation criteria and adjustment scope]"

risks_template:
  - condition: "Evaluation loop optimizes for metrics but degrades unmeasured quality"
    action: "Add qualitative human review as secondary criteria source (source: hybrid)"
    monitor: "Periodic human spot-checks of output quality"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "Max iterations too low — system escalates too often"
    action: "Analyze escalation frequency; increase max_iterations if escalations are resolvable"
    monitor: "Escalation rate per workflow run"
    severity: "medium"

  - condition: "Adjustment scope too narrow — system can't make meaningful improvements"
    action: "Review protected fields; expand scope if safe"
    monitor: "Quality delta per iteration (if delta ≈ 0, scope may be too narrow)"
    severity: "medium"

# ==============================================================================
# REAL-WORLD EXAMPLES
# ==============================================================================

examples:
  - name: "Lead Scoring Pipeline"
    description: "Classification workflow that improves precision over iterations"
    evaluation:
      criteria:
        - metric: "classification_precision"
          target: ">= 0.90"
          source: "automated"
        - metric: "classification_recall"
          target: ">= 0.80"
          source: "automated"
        - metric: "inference_latency"
          target: "p95 < 500ms"
          source: "automated"
      adjustment:
        authority: "ai_autonomous"
        scope: ["model_parameters", "feature_weights", "threshold"]
        protected: ["intent.goal", "constraints", "training_data_source"]
      cycle:
        trigger: "on_completion"
        max_iterations: 5
        stop_when: "precision >= 0.90 AND recall >= 0.80"
        escalate_when: "Cannot reach targets after 5 iterations"

  - name: "ETL Data Quality Pipeline"
    description: "Da

metric: "classification_recall"
          target: ">= 0.80"
          source: "automated"
        - metric: "inference_latency"
          target: "p95 < 500ms"
          source: "automated"
      adjustment:
        authority: "ai_autonomous"
        scope: ["model_parameters", "feature_weights", "threshold"]
        protected: ["intent.goal", "constraints", "training_data_source"]
      cycle:
        trigger: "on_completion"
        max_iterations: 5
        stop_when: "precision >= 0.90 AND recall >= 0.80"
        escalate_when: "Cannot reach targets after 5 iterations"

  - name: "ETL Data Quality Pipeline"
    description: "Data pipeline that improves completeness and accuracy per run"
    evaluation:
      criteria:
        - metric: "data_completeness"
          target: ">= 99%"
          source: "automated"
        - metric: "field_accuracy"
          target: ">= 95%"
          source: "hybrid"
        - metric: "processing_time"
          target: "< 30 minutes"
          source: "automated"
      adjustment:
        authority: "ai_proposed"
        scope: ["parsing_rules", "validation_thresholds", "retry_logic"]
        protected: ["intent.goal", "constraints", "source_schema", "target_schema"]
      cycle:
        trigger: "on_failure"
        max_iterations: 3
        stop_when: "completeness >= 99% AND accuracy >= 95%"
        escalate_when: "Data source schema changed or accuracy < 90% after 3 iterations"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
# ==============================================================================

implementation_pattern:
  directory_structure: |
    workflow/{workflow_name}/
    ├── {workflow_name}_intent.yaml      # Workflow intent (with evaluation + authorship)
    ├── pipeline.py                      # Workflow implementation
    ├── evaluator.py                     # Quality measurement logic
    ├── adjuster.py                      # Adjustment logic (within declared scope)
    ├── intents/                         # Task-level intents (if needed)
    │   └── {step}_intent.yaml
    └── tests/
        ├── test_evaluation.py           # Evaluation loop tests
        ├── test_protected_fields.py     # Protected field enforcement
        └── test_escalation.py           # Escalation mechanism tests

  key_implementation_decisions:
    - decision: "How to measure quality automatically"
      options:
        - "Ground truth comparison (if labeled data exists)"
        - "Heuristic scoring (rule-based quality check)"
        - "LLM-as-judge (AI evaluates output quality)"
      recommendation: "Ground truth when available; heuristic for known patterns; LLM-as-judge as last resort (introduces its own hallucination risk)"

    - decision: "How to enforce protected fields"
      options:
        - "Pre-adjustment validation (check before applying)"
        - "Immutable snapshot (freeze protected fields at start)"
        - "Post-adjustment diff (compare before/after, reject if protected changed)"
      recommendation: "Immutable snapshot — freeze at loop start, compare after each iteration"

# ===============================================

check)"
        - "LLM-as-judge (AI evaluates output quality)"
      recommendation: "Ground truth when available; heuristic for known patterns; LLM-as-judge as last resort (introduces its own hallucination risk)"

    - decision: "How to enforce protected fields"
      options:
        - "Pre-adjustment validation (check before applying)"
        - "Immutable snapshot (freeze protected fields at start)"
        - "Post-adjustment diff (compare before/after, reject if protected changed)"
      recommendation: "Immutable snapshot — freeze at loop start, compare after each iteration"

# ==============================================================================
# BEST PRACTICES
# ==============================================================================

best_practices:
  - "Start with max_iterations: 3 — increase only with evidence that more iterations help"
  - "Protected fields should include everything in the intent section and all constraints"
  - "Use 'ai_proposed' authority first; promote to 'ai_autonomous' only after validation"
  - "Log every adjustment with before/after metrics for auditability"
  - "Include at least one human-reviewable criteria (source: hybrid) for complex outputs"
  - "Evaluation criteria should be stricter than constraints — constraints are the floor, evaluation targets the ceiling"

common_pitfalls:
  - pitfall: "Evaluating metrics the system can game"
    solution: "Include diverse metrics that are hard to optimize simultaneously; add human spot-checks"
  - pitfall: "Too many protected fields — system can't adjust anything meaningful"
    solution: "Protect intent and constraints; allow implementation and process changes"
  - pitfall: "No escalation — system silently fails to improve"
    solution: "Always define escalate_when; test that escalation actually triggers notification"
  - pitfall: "Evaluation criteria conflict with constraints"
    solution: "Evaluation targets must be above constraint thresholds, never below"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION GUIDANCE
# ==============================================================================

verification_pattern:
  evaluation_loop_tests: |
    For each self-evaluating workflow, write tests that verify:
    1. Evaluation loop terminates within max_iterations
    2. Protected fields are not modified by any adjustment
    3. Constraints are not relaxed by any adjustment
    4. Escalation triggers when criteria not met after max_iterations
    5. Quality metrics improve or stay stable (never regress) per iteration

  example_test: |
    def test_evaluation_respects_protected_fields():
        workflow = create_workflow()
        original_goal = workflow.intent["goal"]
        original_constraints = workflow.intent["constraints"]
        workflow.run_evaluation_loop()
        assert workflow.intent["goal"] == original_goal
        assert workflow.intent["constraints"] == original_constraints

    def test_evaluation_terminates():
        workflow = create_workflow(max_iterations=3)
        result = workflow.run_evaluation_loop()
        assert result.iterations_run <= 3

    def test_escalation

rove or stay stable (never regress) per iteration

  example_test: |
    def test_evaluation_respects_protected_fields():
        workflow = create_workflow()
        original_goal = workflow.intent["goal"]
        original_constraints = workflow.intent["constraints"]
        workflow.run_evaluation_loop()
        assert workflow.intent["goal"] == original_goal
        assert workflow.intent["constraints"] == original_constraints

    def test_evaluation_terminates():
        workflow = create_workflow(max_iterations=3)
        result = workflow.run_evaluation_loop()
        assert result.iterations_run <= 3

    def test_escalation_triggers():
        workflow = create_workflow(max_iterations=1)
        workflow.set_unreachable_target("precision >= 1.0")
        result = workflow.run_evaluation_loop()
        assert result.escalated is True

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Initial recipe for self-evaluating workflow pattern"
    reason: "Workflows with measurable quality needed a structured improvement loop beyond binary verification"
